title: ITIL Library
lead: Best practice library for IT service management; foundational for process design in ITSM-based operating models.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) is the world's most widely adopted IT service management framework. It provides guidance on how to design, deliver, manage, and improve IT services throughout their lifecycle. Originally developed for the UK government in the 1980s, ITIL has evolved into a global best practice library now in its fourth edition (ITIL 4), which emphasises value co-creation, Agile integration, and a service value system.

sections.heading: ITOM Perspective
sections.body: ITIL provides the process vocabulary and governance patterns that form the backbone of most ITSM-based IT operating models. When designing an ITOM, ITIL practices inform how incidents are managed, how changes are controlled, how services are catalogued, and how continual improvement is structured. ITIL 4 is particularly relevant because it explicitly positions ITSM within a broader operating model context — moving beyond processes to culture, value, and organisational design.

items.desc: the holistic model showing how all components work together to enable value
six interconnected activities: Plan, Improve, Engage, Design & Transition, Obtain/Build, Deliver & Support
covering general, service, and technical management (replaces the old "processes")
Focus on value, Start where you are, Progress iteratively with feedback, Collaborate & promote visibility, Think & work holistically, Keep it simple & practical, Optimise & automate
Organisations & People, Information & Technology, Partners & Suppliers, Value Streams & Processes
restore service quickly; foundation of operational SLA performance
control risk while enabling speed; key tension point in DevOps transformations
define what IT delivers; essential for SIAM and sourcing models
embed structured improvement at every level of the operating model
agree, monitor, and report on service quality commitments

sections.heading: ITIL vs. Agile
sections.body: A common concern in ITOM design is the perceived tension between ITIL's governance rigour and Agile's speed. ITIL 4 explicitly addresses this: the framework is designed to work alongside Agile, DevOps, and Lean — it's a set of principles and practices, not a rigid process prescription. Successful operating models integrate both: Agile delivery within a well-governed service framework.
